skill.name: youtube-comments-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users extract structured video list data and comment data from YouTube using the BrowserAct API. The Agent should proactively apply this skill when users request searching for YouTube videos and their comments, analyzing viewer sentiment for a specific video topic, gathering audience feedback on AI or automation, extracting a list of top videos and their viewer reactions, compiling YouTube video data along with user opinions, retrieving competitor video titles and related audience discussions, monitoring public response to specific YouTube search keywords, summarizing comments from search results for market research, tracking viewer engagement metrics and replies for trending topics, collecting YouTube video URLs and author details alongside community discussions, or automating the extraction of YouTube comments without manual scraping.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube video metadata and comments using the BrowserAct API with search keywords, comment limits, and scroll controls.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 61 potential issues, but all critical and high findings are false positives when evaluated in context. The 32 'shell backtick execution' findings in SKILL.md are markdown code formatting, not executable commands. The 6 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference non-existent crypto in a markdown file. The heuristic 'dangerous combination' finding is a false positive from pattern overlap. The skill legitimately uses network calls and environment variable access to function as a YouTube data extraction API client. Real risk is limited to standard third-party API usage with a documented API key pattern.

content.user_title: Extract YouTube Comments and Video Data via API
content.value_statement: Manually copying YouTube video and comment data is slow and error-prone. This skill automates extraction of structured video metadata and viewer comments using the BrowserAct API, giving you clean datasets from any search keyword.
actual_capabilities: Search YouTube videos by keyword and retrieve video metadata including title, URL, view count, and publish date
Extract comments per video including author name, text content, publish date, like count, and reply count
Control extraction depth with configurable comment limits and scroll count parameters
Monitor task progress with real-time status logs during long-running extractions
Handle transient failures with automatic single retry before reporting errors
Output structured data ready for analysis, reporting, or integration into other tools
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key, not a YouTube API key
Extraction speed depends on the remote BrowserAct service, not local processing power
Cannot access age-restricted, private, or deleted YouTube content
Comment volume is limited to what YouTube displays for each video rather than all existing comments

use_cases.title: Market Research and Competitor Analysis
use_cases.description: Collect video metadata and audience comments for competitor YouTube channels to identify trends, sentiment, and engagement patterns.

use_cases.title: Content Strategy and Audience Feedback
use_cases.description: Gather viewer reactions and comments on trending topics to inform content creation and understand audience preferences.
use_cases.target_user: Content Creator or Social Media Manager

use_cases.title: Product Feedback Mining
use_cases.description: Extract feature requests, bug reports, and user pain points from product-related YouTube video comments for product teams.

prompt_templates.title: Basic keyword search with comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube for videos about {keyword} and return the video list with their comments.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants quick results for a simple keyword search

prompt_templates.title: Controlled extraction with limits
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the top 10 videos for '{keyword}' and extract up to 20 comments per video. Sort by view count.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs a moderate-sized dataset with control over volume

prompt_templates.title: Deep extraction with scrolling
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for '{keyword}', get 5 videos with 50 comments each, and scroll 3 times in the comments section to load more comments before extraction.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs deeper comment data from popular videos

prompt_templates.title: Multi-keyword batch for comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Run extractions for each of these keywords: {keyword1}, {keyword2}, {keyword3}. For each keyword, get 5 videos with 30 comments. Present the results as a comparison table with video titles, top comments, and engagement metrics.
prompt_templates.scenario: An analyst compares audience discussions across multiple topics

output_examples.input: Search YouTube for videos about AI automation and show their top comments
output_examples.output: A table of videos with titles, URLs, view counts, and publish dates. Each video is followed by a list of its top comments showing the commenter name, comment text, like count, and reply count.

output_examples.input: Get the top 5 videos about web scraping with 15 comments each
output_examples.output: A structured report listing each video with its title, URL, and view count. Under each video, comments are listed with author name, text content, publication time, and likes.

output_examples.input: Compare audience reactions to 'ChatGPT vs Claude' across 10 videos
output_examples.output: A comparison table with video metadata columns (title, views, publish date) and a comments section for each video showing the most-liked comments and total reply counts per comment.

best_practices: Start with small comment limits (5-10) and low scroll counts (1-2) to verify data quality before running larger extractions
Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before invoking the skill to avoid runtime errors
Monitor terminal status logs during execution to distinguish normal progress from actual failures
anti_patterns: Do not run the script without setting BROWSERACT_API_KEY first, as the script will immediately exit with an error
Do not set excessively high scroll counts above 10, as this increases execution time without meaningful data gain
Do not ignore Invalid authorization errors by retrying; this indicates an expired or incorrect API key and requires user action

faq.question: What is the BROWSERACT_API_KEY and where do I get it?
faq.answer: It is your personal API key for the BrowserAct service. You can obtain it from the BrowserAct Console at browseract.com.

faq.question: Does this skill require a YouTube API key?
faq.answer: No. It uses the BrowserAct service to access YouTube data. You only need a valid BrowserAct API key.

faq.question: How long does a typical extraction take?
faq.answer: It depends on the number of comments and scroll count. Most tasks complete within 1 to 5 minutes.

faq.question: Can I search for any YouTube keyword with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Any YouTube search keyword works. Results match what YouTube displays for that search query.

faq.question: What happens if the extraction task fails?
faq.answer: The script retries once automatically. If it fails again, it stops and reports the error message for diagnosis.

faq.question: Does this skill violate YouTube terms of service?
faq.answer: This skill accesses YouTube data through the BrowserAct service. Review YouTube terms for automated data collection rules.
